reasoning_model_1: The user explicitly requested the sentence to be read with a 'whisper'. Analyzing the audio at 0:00-0:02, Model 1 delivers the line with a loud, projected voice, resembling a shout or an urgent call. reasoning_model_2: The user explicitly requested a 'whisper'. Analyzing the audio at 0:00-0:04, Model 2 reads the text with a standard, fully voiced tone at a normal conversation volume. model_comparison: Both models completely failed to adhere to the core instruction 'read... with a whisper'. A whisper requires a specific paralinguistic change involving breathy phonation and reduced volume. Model 1 produced a loud, urgent voice (effectively a shout), which is the polar opposite of a whisper in terms of volume dynamics. Model 2 produced a standard, robotic read at normal volume, also devoid of any whispering characteristics. Since neither model demonstrated even a partial attempt to modify the volume or vocal texture to match the prompt, both receive the lowest possible score for this dimension. It is a tie at 0.
